name: Illescas and Alameda de la Sagra –
name_original: Illescas y Alameda de la Sagra – circular desde Seseña
description: This moderate 37.5-mile road cycling route from Seseña explores the arid plains and historic sites of Illescas and Alameda de la Sagra.
region_intro: Road cycling routes near Seseña traverse arid plains and agricultural land, providing open roads and expansive views. The region features varied terrain with rolling hills and moderate elevation changes, suitable for different skill levels. While extensive mountainous climbs are not prominent, the broader area offers routes with elevation gains. Nearby, riverside paths along the Tajo River near Aranjuez provide scenic cycling opportunities. Best road cycling routes around Seseña The most popular road cycling route is Illescas and Alameda de la Sagra –, a 37.5 miles (60.4 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 4 minutes to complete. This route traverses the region's characteristic arid plains and agricultural areas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Road to Aranjuez – Titulcia Main Square loop from Seseña (Cerrada Temporalmente), a moderate 37.5 miles (60.4 km) path. This route offers a journey towards Aranjuez, featuring varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Plaza Mayor de Chinchón – Titulcia Main Square loop from Polígono Industrial La Sendilla, a 33.8 miles (54.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain with moderate elevation changes, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Road cycling around Seseña is defined by arid plains, agricultural land, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from endurance rides across flat sections to more challenging routes with elevation gains. The routes in Seseña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 45 reviews. More than 2,600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Seseña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Seseña?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Seseña, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 45 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Seseña?
A: Road cycling routes near Seseña primarily feature extensive arid plains and agricultural areas, providing open roads and expansive views. You'll also encounter rolling hills and moderate elevation changes, suitable for those seeking a more challenging ride without extreme mountainous conditions. For example, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025406580/illescas-and-alameda-de-la-sagra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Illescas and Alameda de la Sagra&lt;/a&gt; traverse these characteristic dry landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes with significant elevation gain for more challenging rides?
A: While Seseña itself doesn't feature extensive mountainous climbs, the broader region offers routes with moderate elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/16067051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor de Chinchón – Titulcia Main Square loop from Polígono Industrial La Sendilla&lt;/a&gt; includes varied terrain with notable elevation changes, providing a good workout.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes around Seseña offer opportunities to explore historic sites and natural features. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1393652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Teja Spring and the Hermitage of San Isidro&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3022680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Titulcia Main Square&lt;/a&gt;. Near Aranjuez, you can find the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4576777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2561736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Chapel of San Isidro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic routes that follow rivers or natural trails?
A: Yes, while many routes cross arid plains, the area near Aranjuez, a short distance from Seseña, offers scenic riverside paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31910513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road to Aranjuez – Titulcia Main Square loop from Seseña&lt;/a&gt; provides varied scenery, including sections that contrast with the dry landscapes, potentially near the Tajo River.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Seseña?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in Seseña, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Cyclists often praise the expansive views across arid plains and agricultural land, the variety of terrain with rolling hills, and the opportunity to visit historic towns along the way.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Seseña?
A: Yes, Seseña offers several easy road cycling routes, with 4 routes specifically categorized as easy. These routes are ideal for riders looking for endurance training or a less strenuous ride across flatter sections of the region's plains.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by lakes or water features?
A: While Seseña is known for its arid plains, there are nearby water features that some routes may approach or offer views of. You can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5225122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Seca de las Esteras&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/407021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinescos Pond&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5144487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de San Juan&lt;/a&gt; in the broader area, which offer a different natural landscape.

Q: Are there any routes that include historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some routes offer glimpses of historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puñoenrostro Castle loop from Seseña&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you past the historic Puñoenrostro Castle, allowing for a cultural stop during your ride.

Q: What is the typical duration for a road cycling route in Seseña?
A: The duration of road cycling routes in Seseña varies depending on the distance and your pace. Many popular routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025406580/illescas-and-alameda-de-la-sagra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Illescas and Alameda de la Sagra&lt;/a&gt; route, are around 37.5 miles (60.4 km) and typically take about 4 hours to complete. Shorter options are also available for quicker rides.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Jarama River?
A: Yes, the broader region includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7217229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarama River in the Southeast Regional Park&lt;/a&gt;. While not directly within Seseña, some routes in the vicinity might offer views or access to this river, providing a change of scenery from the arid plains.

Q: What kind of cycling events or activities are available in Seseña?
A: Seseña is developing its recreational offerings, including a 'Parque Multiaventura' (Multi-adventure Park) in the 'El Velascón' area. This park caters to outdoor enthusiasts with activities like 'Bike Xtrem circuits' and guided bike rides, indicating a growing commitment to cycling and adventure sports within the municipality.
tips:
Puñoenrostro Castle is an impressive medieval fortress located in Seseña (Toledo), although it is also associated with Torrejón de Velasco (Madrid) due to its proximity and recent restoration projects.
Built in the 14th century, it was granted by Henry IV to Diego Arias de Ávila, his accountant and secretary.
His descendants were named Counts of Puñoenrostro under Charles I.
In 1862, the castle was sold along with its pastureland.

Due to its proximity to Madrid (which is at the same distance as Toledo), Illescas is one of the fastest growing municipalities in the province of Toledo. Even so, its historic center with its narrow streets remains a quiet place to sneak away and enjoy the Puerta de Ugena de Alfonso VI, the church of the Asunción from the 13th century, and the Hospital de Nuestra Señora de La Caridad with five paintings of El Greek.

The Plaza Mayor of Alameda de la Sagra is the social and cultural heart of the town, a space that blends tradition and everyday life.

Located in the historic center of the town, it is surrounded by charming streets and traditional buildings.

It is a meeting point for locals and visitors alike, with bars, restaurants, and shops offering typical cuisine.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Illescas and Alameda de la Sagra circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Seseña. While specific parking areas are not detailed, you can typically find parking in the town of Seseña itself, near the start of the loop, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling trail, meaning it features mostly paved surfaces. However, be aware that some segments may be unpaved, adding a varied and potentially challenging element to the ride. It's classified as a moderate ride, suitable for cyclists with a good fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Castile-La Mancha can experience hot summers. For a more comfortable ride, consider cycling during the spring or autumn months when temperatures are milder. This avoids the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Illescas and Alameda de la Sagra circular route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can explore several points of interest. These include the historic Puñoenrostro Castle, the Historic Center of Illescas, and the Plaza Mayor of Alameda de la Sagra. The general region offers views of arid plains and countryside.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' for racebike cycling. It covers approximately 60.4 kilometers with about 430 meters of elevation gain. While not extremely difficult, it requires a good level of fitness and is generally not recommended for absolute beginners due to its length and potential unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the towns of Illescas and Alameda de la Sagra, as well as starting from Seseña. These towns offer opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, or shops where you can stop for refreshments and breaks.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several local paths. You will encounter sections of the Camino de Alameda, Camino de las Águilas, Camino de Yeles a Realejo, Camino del Cairén, and the Cañada Real de las Merinas, among others.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this racebike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and moderate difficulty, it's advisable to pack essentials such as water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes and a pump), appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Consider bringing a GPS device or using the Komoot app for navigation.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this 60.4-kilometer racebike route is approximately 4 hours. This can vary depending on your fitness level, speed, and the number of stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: Is the Illescas and Alameda de la Sagra circular route family-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, length of over 60 km, and potential unpaved sections, this route is generally not considered ideal for young children or families looking for a leisurely ride. It's better suited for experienced cyclists.
